avoid: Do not optimize based on speed because there is no valid benchmark for an incorrect candidate.
Do not add handwritten full GEMM kernels for W @ X.
Do not hardcode d=4096 or any hidden dimension-specific assumption.
Do not materialize B.T as a separate transpose-copy.
Do not use result caching unless the benchmark explicitly defines repeated identical inputs as the target workload.
Do not promote based on the single passing 4096 shape while 3584 and 4608 fail.
evidence: Correctness failed overall, so performance cannot be evaluated or promoted.

Shapes 3584 and 4608 fail correctness while 4096 passes; this suggests a shape-dependent cuBLAS layout/leading-dimension/op-flag issue or a numerical-mode mismatch rather than a general speed improvement.
Outputs are finite and relative L2 error is small, but max absolute error reaches 0.037109375 at d=4608, exceeding the correctness threshold.
Benchmark result is unavailable because correctness failed.
The candidate is intended to be a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM path, which is the right optimization direction, but it must first be repaired and validated for all runtime d values.

recommended_actions: check rank-16 indexing and vector alignment
Do not promote this candidate until correctness passes for 3584, 4096, and 4608.
Audit the row-major-to-column-major cuBLAS mapping for all three SGEMMs: op flags, m/n/k, lda/ldb/ldc, and alpha/beta values.
Use direct cuBLAS SGEMM for the main term W @ X; do not replace the dense d x d GEMM with a custom kernel.
Keep the low-rank path as cuBLAS SGEMMs rather than handwritten rank-16 kernels.
Avoid explicit B.T materialization. Compute a temporary U with logical row-major shape d x 16 representing U = X.T @ B, equivalent to U.T = B.T @ X.
Then accumulate the low-rank term directly into Y with beta = 1 using a final rank-16 SGEMM for Y += A @ (B.T @ X).

A plausible cuBLAS row-major mapping is: main Y = W @ X via column-major view using A=X, B=W, C=Y with opN/opN and dimensions d,d,d; low-rank U using B with opN and X with opT to produce U storage d x 16; final update using U with opT and A with opN, m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1.
Check whether cuBLAS math mode matches the reference. If the reference expects strict FP32, disable TF32 or use a pedantic FP32 compute mode before benchmarking.
Re-run correctness before collecting benchmark data.
